runs.note: Measured before the bridge-aligned regional classical path landed in batch_analyze. Superseded by the 2026-04-09 v2 rerun.
runs.notes: First Chopin run. Low alignment rate (11.3%) expected — Chopin annotations are sparser than Bach (1-2 per measure in 3/4 vs many per measure in SATB). Root agreement 60.0% on aligned regions. BIR% 77.2% (higher than Bach/Beethoven — piano bass voice). Lydian 4.2% of regions — real Lydian passages confirmed (modal calibration target). Script: run_chopin_validation.py (new).

runs.note: Measured before the bridge-aligned regional classical path landed in batch_analyze. Superseded by the 2026-04-09 v2 rerun.
runs.notes: First Grieg run. Alignment 42.2% (better than Chopin). Root agreement 54.8% — lowest so far, reflects late-romantic chromatic voice leading. BIR% 67.1% (lower than Chopin, higher than Mozart). Lydian 11.9% — much higher than Chopin (4.2%), expected for Grieg (Norwegian folk influence, raised 4th in major). Mixolydian 8.6%, Dorian 5.2% — both confirmed. Script: run_grieg_validation.py (new).

runs.note: Measured before the bridge-aligned regional classical path landed in batch_analyze. Superseded by the 2026-04-09 v2 rerun.
runs.notes: First Schumann run. Small corpus (13 pieces). BIR% 91.7% — highest of any corpus, consistent with dense Romantic piano texture and short harmonic regions. Alignment 24.3%. Modal distribution near-diatonic: major 59.6%, minor 33.1%, very few non-Ionian modes (Lydian 2.2%, Dorian 1.5%). Script: run_schumann_validation.py (new).

runs.note: Measured before the bridge-aligned regional classical path landed in batch_analyze. Superseded by the 2026-04-09 v2 rerun.
runs.notes: First Tchaikovsky run. Small corpus (12 pieces). Root agreement 63.9%. BIR% 49.3% — notably lower than other piano corpora (Chopin 77.2%, Grieg 67.1%), suggesting more correct inversions identified or different bass-writing style. Lydian 6.3%, Dorian+Mixolydian 5.5% — moderate modal presence. Script: run_tchaikovsky_validation.py (new).

runs.note: Measured before the bridge-aligned regional classical path landed in batch_analyze. Superseded by the 2026-04-09 v2 rerun.
runs.notes: First Dvorak run. Small corpus (12 pieces). Root agreement 66.9%. BIR% 70.8%. Alignment 50.0% — best of any small corpus. Notable: minor (44.1%) exceeds major (32.4%) — Dvorak's Slavic folk influence heavily weighted toward minor. Harmonic minor at 7.6% — high, reflects Dvorak's use of raised 7th in minor. Lydian nearly absent (1.0%). Script: run_dvorak_validation.py (new).

First Bach suites run. Agreement 66.7% � notably below Bach chorales (83.7%). Many movements return 0 regions: dance forms (Allemandes, Courantes, Gigues) are typically 2-voice or sparse, below the 3-PC minimum. Alignment 32.1% � sparse DCML annotations for dance texture. Modal variety: Dorian 9.5%, Mixolydian 7.3%, Phrygian 6.4% � richer than chorales. Script: run_bach_suites_validation.py (new).

runs.note: Measured with onset-only path 3 against music21 surface labels. Both methodology and algorithm were incorrect.
runs.notes: Preset sensitivity check 1. Baroque preset vs Standard baseline (83.7%). Result: 83.7% — identical to Standard. Baroque preset does not degrade chord identity on Bach chorales. Expected: SATB texture is strongly major/minor; mode priors have no effect when evidence is unambiguous. Script: run_bach_preset.py --preset Baroque --skip-cpp.

Preset sensitivity check 2. Modal preset vs Standard baseline (54.8%). Result: 54.8% — identical to Standard. Modal preset does not improve or degrade chord identity agreement. Modal distribution shifts significantly: Lydian 11.9%→21.6% (+9.7 pp), Major 53.6%→43.8% (−9.8 pp), Mixolydian 8.6%→9.9% (+1.3 pp), Dorian 5.2%→6.7% (+1.5 pp). The 81 extra Lydian detections and 31 extra Mixolydian are predominantly in unaligned regions (not compared against DCML), leaving agreement unchanged. Script: run_grieg_validation.py --ours-dir tools/corpus_grieg_modal.

Deferred corpus. All 66 movements return 0 regions: CPE Bach keyboard sonatas are single-voice melody lines per staff (85-97% single-note chord elements). Four staves never accumulate 3 simultaneous pitch classes. Empfindsamer Stil implies harmony clearly in single lines � excellent future validation target once melodic/arpeggio inference is implemented. Do NOT remove from roadmap. Script: run_cpe_bach_validation.py (new).

INVALIDATED on 2026-04-07. This run measured a circular path: the old §4.1c jazz implementation copied the written chord-symbol root into the analysis result, so root agreement was not a real analyzer measurement. Retained for audit history only; superseded by 20260407_205723.

Corrected Phase 1a monophonic-jazz validation after removing the circular written-root override from the §4.1c jazz path. Chord symbols define region boundaries and comparison metadata only; `rootPitchClass` now comes from note-based analysis on `collectRegionTones()`. Agreement is 18.0% on analyzable regions (605/3361). 1103 regions produce no analyzed chord because they contain fewer than 3 distinct pitch classes. Dominant mismatch pattern is functional reinterpretation (e.g. written F -> analyzed C/Am/Gm, written Bb -> analyzed F/Gm/Dm/C), indicating the current vertical analyzer is a poor fit for monophonic jazz melody even with correct region boundaries.

Effendi follow-up corpus after Omnibook Phase 1a. Downloaded 368 XML files, filtered to 292 qualifying single-part scores with at least one harmony tag and at least 10 notes, and analyzed all 292 successfully after fixing a MusicXML pass-1 divide-by-zero triggered by II__Arriving%20Soon.xml. Aggregate root agreement remains 27.9% on analyzable chord-symbol regions (1119/4010) after backfilling the previously crashing file, so the importer fix did not materially change corpus behavior. 5864 chord-symbol regions produced no analyzed chord because they remained below the current vertical analyzer's evidence threshold. Breakdown warnings in the saved report indicate a few files where harmony-event count does not exactly match fromChordSymbol region count.

Preset sensitivity check on the corrected Omnibook Phase 1a corpus. Standard preset scores 18.1% root agreement (608/3361) versus the Jazz preset baseline of 18.0% (605/3361), a +3-region / +0.09 pp change that is within corpus noise. No-analyzed-chord regions remain identical at 1103, so preset choice does not explain the monophonic-jazz failure mode; the limiting factor is still the current vertical analyzer operating on single-line melody.

Corrected full Rampage Swing baseline with the two MXL-only charts added directly to batch_analyze. compare_omnibook.py now infers Rampageswing source directories, reads .mxl source files, and prefers source MusicXML kind tags over the lossy JSON writtenQuality field, restoring Dominant7 / Major7 / Minor7 counts in the breakdown. Result: 39.8% root agreement (691/1735) on 33 chord-symbol-bearing charts. Zero-harmony XML charts remain excluded from this baseline because they contribute no comparable chord-symbol regions.

First blanket jazz-scoring experiment using ChordTemporalContext::jazzMode plus three new preferences: jazzBassRootMultiplier=0.25, jazzGuideToneBonus=0.8, jazzSus4Penalty=0.5. Result regressed the corrected full Rampage Swing baseline from 39.8% (691/1735) to 35.1% (609/1735), despite local improvements on a few hard charts such as jumpin_woodside_4horn (13% -> 21%). Dominant7 improved slightly (36.5% -> 38.6%), but Major, Major7, Minor7, and Diminished broke badly. Conclusion: blanket bass-root downscaling across all chord-symbol-driven regions is too blunt and should not be adopted as-is.

runs.note: Measured with onset-only path 3 against music21 surface labels. Both methodology and algorithm were incorrect.
runs.notes: Safety check after the blanket jazz-scoring experiment. Bach chorale chord identity remained exactly 83.7% (3397/4058 aligned regions), matching the established §4.1c baseline. The lower 38.3% figure in the HTML summary is the broader full-agreement metric (root+quality+Roman+key) and is not comparable to the chord-identity baseline. This confirms the jazzMode gate did not leak into non-chord-symbol corpora; the Rampageswing regression is due to the jazz scoring design itself, not accidental activation on classical scores.

Full Rampageswing polyphonic-jazz corpus run. Of 36 requested charts, 34 were available as XML and 2 were MXL-only (nostalgia_in_times_square_4horn, police_squad_4horn). Three XML charts contained no harmony tags and were skipped (begin_the_beguine_4horn, big_apple_contest_4horn, one_oclock_jump_4horn), leaving 31 processed charts. Aggregate root agreement is 40.7% (658/1618) on comparable chord-symbol regions with 744 no-analyzed-chord regions. Diagnostic follow-up on Jumpin' at the Woodside showed that in failing jazz regions the written root is often absent and the lowest note is typically the 5th or 2nd/9th rather than the root, confirming that the current bass-root bias is actively harmful for jazz voicings. Breakdown on the full corpus: Major 40.5%, Minor 40.4%, HalfDiminished 53.3%, Diminished 41.0%.

runs.status: complete - mixed result, thresholds not met
runs.notes: Selective follow-up experiment after reverting the blanket jazz bass-root penalty. Kept bassNoteRootBonus unchanged and added only two jazzMode-gated contextual adjustments: dominant tritone bonus (jazzGuideToneBonus=0.8 when major 3rd + minor 7th are both present for a dominant-seventh candidate) and Suspended4 penalty (jazzSus4Penalty=0.5 when the 3rd is absent). Aggregate Rampage Swing root agreement improved slightly from the corrected 39.8% baseline (691/1735) to 39.9% (693/1735), and Dominant7 improved strongly from 36.5% to 42.3%. Major7 held exactly at its 24.4% baseline floor. However Minor7 regressed from 38.5% to 31.8% and Diminished regressed from 40.7% to 32.2%, so the run does not satisfy the acceptance criteria. Conclusion: the guide-tone signal is real, but the current bonus magnitude still distorts non-dominant quality competition and needs another iteration before adoption.

Safety check for the selective guide-tone follow-up. Bach chorale chord identity remained exactly 83.7% (3397/4058 aligned regions), matching the established baseline and staying within the requested ±0.5pp envelope. This confirms the jazzMode-gated dominant tritone bonus and targeted Suspended4 penalty remain isolated to chord-symbol-driven analysis and do not perturb the non-jazz corpus benchmark.

Batch-only diagnostic run using --inject-written-root. For each fromChordSymbol jazz region, batch_analyze injected a synthetic low written-root tone before calling analyzeChord(), simulating the missing bass-player root in horn-only charts. Rampage Swing root agreement jumped from the corrected 39.8% baseline (691/1735) to 98.3% (1784/1815), with every tracked quality rising into the 96.8%–100.0% range (Major 98.5%, Dominant7 98.9%, Major7 100.0%, Minor7 96.8%, Diminished 96.8%). Comparable-region count also increased because many formerly 2-note regions crossed the 3-pitch-class analysis threshold once the synthetic root was present. This strongly confirms that missing bass/root material is the dominant failure mode in the Rampage Swing horn-only texture; the vertical analyzer behaves almost perfectly when given the root that a real rhythm section would supply. Diagnostic only: no bridge code changed, and the flag is confined to batch_analyze.

Batch-only diagnostic run using --inject-written-root on the Omnibook monophonic jazz corpus. Injecting a synthetic low written-root tone before analysis raised root agreement from the corrected Jazz baseline of 18.0% (605/3361) to 99.9% (3675/3677). Comparable-region count increased because many melody-only 1- or 2-note regions became analyzable once the synthetic root was added. This confirms that the Omnibook failure mode is also root identification from incomplete vertical material rather than a downstream formatting or comparison problem. As with Rampage Swing, this was a diagnostic experiment only: the analyzer still produced the result normally from the augmented tone set, and the flag is isolated to batch_analyze.

runs.git_hash: 6c8590d8e0 (+ uncommitted valid-root chord-symbol gate fixes in batch_analyze and notation bridge)
runs.notes: Post-fix local-score sweep after two changes: (1) the chord-symbol gate now requires at least one Harmony annotation with rootTpc != TPC_INVALID before activating the chord-symbol-driven jazz path, and (2) batch_analyze now parses command-line paths via Qt Unicode arguments and reads/writes paths via QString/QFile on Windows. This eliminated the systemic When in Rome zero-region failures for Quartets (now 70/70 with regions) and Piano Sonatas (54/54 with regions), and reduced the prior non-ASCII-path load failures from 210 files to a single remaining crash on OpenScore-LiederCorpus/Chaminade,_Cécile/_/Souhait/score.mxl (exit code 0xC0000005). The output directory is intentionally the user-requested fixed path tools/corpus_when_in_rome rather than a timestamped corpus directory.

runs.git_hash: b0e4a5b456 (+ uncommitted: bridge-aligned classical batch path, Bach baseline docs)
runs.notes: Chopin v2 rerun after the batch classical path was aligned to the live notation bridge (regional accumulation, Jaccard boundaries, bridge-like temporal context and key fallback). Headline root agreement dropped from 60.0% to 57.5%, but aligned coverage increased from 11.3% to 41.7% and 53 of 55 movements gained aligned regions, so this is not a simple like-for-like regression. The corrected path surfaces many more structurally comparable DCML beats; remaining weakness is still root identification in dense piano textures.

runs.git_hash: b0e4a5b456 (+ uncommitted: bridge-aligned classical batch path, Bach baseline docs)
runs.notes: Grieg v2 rerun on the bridge-aligned regional classical path. Agreement dropped from 54.8% to 47.3% while alignment moved from 42.2% to 39.5%. This suggests the old onset-only path was flattering Grieg relative to the live bridge, and the remaining gap is genuine analyzer difficulty in chromatic late-Romantic piano writing rather than a batch-only divergence.

runs.git_hash: b0e4a5b456 (+ uncommitted: bridge-aligned classical batch path, Bach baseline docs)
runs.notes: Schumann v2 rerun on the bridge-aligned regional classical path. Agreement improved slightly from 63.6% to 64.0% while alignment increased from 24.3% to 42.1%, indicating that the corrected regional path is at least neutral here and materially improves comparison coverage.

runs.git_hash: b0e4a5b456 (+ uncommitted: bridge-aligned classical batch path, corpus v2 docs)
runs.notes: Tchaikovsky v2 rerun on the bridge-aligned regional classical path. Agreement dropped from 63.9% to 58.1%, alignment dropped from 42.4% to 36.0%, and bassIsRoot in disagreements rose from 49.3% to 68.1%, moving Tchaikovsky into the same broad bass-root-bias cluster as the other non-Bach classical corpora.

runs.git_hash: b0e4a5b456 (+ uncommitted: bridge-aligned classical batch path, corpus v2 docs)
runs.notes: Dvorak v2 rerun on the bridge-aligned regional classical path. Agreement improved slightly from 66.9% to 68.0% while alignment slipped from 50.0% to 45.7%. Dvorak remains one of the stronger Romantic piano corpora, but its disagreement pattern still shows substantial bass-root bias at 67.6%.

runs.git_hash: b0e4a5b456 (+ uncommitted: bridge-aligned classical batch path, corpus v2 docs)
runs.notes: Mozart v2 rerun on the bridge-aligned regional classical path. Agreement dropped from 67.5% to 56.7% and alignment dropped from 52.6% to 39.8%. One movement (`K533-3`) failed in batch_analyze again, so the v2 aggregate remains 53 processed movements, matching the earlier baseline size. bassIsRoot in disagreements climbed from 38.6% to 68.3%, which is a major shift toward the broader cross-corpus bass-root-bias pattern.

runs.git_hash: b0e4a5b456 (+ uncommitted: bridge-aligned classical batch path, corpus v2 docs)
runs.notes: Corelli v2 rerun on the bridge-aligned regional classical path. Agreement dropped slightly from 65.5% to 63.9% and alignment from 35.8% to 33.5%, but the key diagnostic is bassIsRoot in disagreements rising to 87.0%. This is the strongest cross-corpus signal that the current bass-root bias is not just a piano-texture issue.

runs.git_hash: b0e4a5b456 (+ uncommitted: bridge-aligned classical batch path, corpus v2 docs)
runs.notes: Beethoven v2 rerun on the bridge-aligned regional classical path. Agreement dropped from 62.2% to 57.1%, alignment from 41.6% to 39.3%, and bassIsRoot in disagreements rose to 68.2%. Because Beethoven is a chamber corpus rather than a piano corpus, this supports bass-root bias as a general scoring issue rather than an Alberti-bass-only artifact.

runs.git_hash: 6c8590d8e0 (+ uncommitted compare_when_in_rome.py and prior When in Rome gate/path fixes)
runs.k311_skip: Mozart K311/2 analysis.txt fails music21 parse (too many notes in m1), permanent skip
runs.missing_analysis: 425 of 760 files have no local analysis.txt (remote.json only, not downloaded)
runs.notes: Comparison run using tools/compare_when_in_rome.py. Unlike earlier DCML TSV workflows, this corpus is compared by parsing adjacent analysis.txt RomanText files through music21, resolving each RomanNumeral to an absolute root pitch class in its current inline key context, converting the RomanText offset to MuseScore ticks via getOffsetInHierarchy(score) * 480, and matching annotations against analyzed regions by containment in [startTick, endTick) with a half-beat fallback tolerance. A pre-run diagnostic on Mozart K279/1 showed the high unmatched rate is caused by genuine coverage gaps rather than a broken tick formula: the score spans the full annotation tick range, but only 8 analyzed regions were emitted across 7 measures, leaving many annotation points in uncovered gaps. One file failed at RomanText parse time (Piano_Sonatas/Mozart,_Wolfgang_Amadeus/K311/2/analysis.txt) because music21 raised RomanTextTranslateException: too many notes in measure m1.

runs.git_hash: b0e4a5b456 (+ uncommitted: batch classical bridge-alignment port, Rule 10 docs, compare_bach_wir.py)

aggregate.surface_reference_type: music21 overlap against dense surface labels

runs.notes: Structural Bach comparison using the new tools/compare_bach_wir.py script against local When in Rome analysis.txt files, not the music21 overlap comparator. The Bach-specific local DCML TSV tree contains metadata and measure tables only; the usable structural reference is the local WIR RomanText corpus. Matching is annotation-centric: for each WIR annotation, parse analysis.txt via music21 RomanText, resolve its absolute root pitch class, convert the annotation offset to ticks, and compare against the analyzed region active at that tick with the existing half-beat nearest-start fallback. Coverage is effectively complete once an analysis.txt exists: 18357/18466 annotations matched a region, only 109 no-match annotations (0.6%). Root agreement is 50.0% on matched annotations and 49.7% over all annotations. This was the first bridge-aligned Bach structural baseline and is now superseded by the post-bassfix run. Retain the 38.0% music21-overlap figure only as a secondary surface-label reference; it is not an appropriate structural-accuracy baseline because the live product now emits structural regions rather than music21-style dense surface labels.

runs.git_hash: f29d85debf (+ uncommitted: bassNoteRootBonus tiered support conditioning, pending docs/registry update)
runs.notes: Post-bassfix Bach structural rerun after conditioning bassNoteRootBonus on root-position support in the accumulated tones. Root agreement improves from 50.0% to 51.5% on matched WIR annotations, with coverage unchanged in practice (0.6% no-match). This is the current Bach structural benchmark.

runs.git_hash: f29d85debf (+ uncommitted: bassNoteRootBonus tiered support conditioning, pending docs/registry update)
runs.notes: Post-bassfix Beethoven rerun. Agreement improves from 57.1% to 59.5% while bassIsRoot in disagreements drops from 68.2% to 51.8%, confirming that the fix helps a chamber corpus as well as the piano corpora.

Official direct-corpus baseline after the DCML comparator refresh now applies relativeroot when computing reference root_pc for applied chords. Dvorak rises from the earlier 66.2%/65.9% checkpoint family to 79.2%.
Official direct-corpus baseline after the comparator refresh and fresh 2026-04-11 rerun. Schumann settles at 61.6% on 13 movements.
Official direct-corpus baseline after the fresh 2026-04-11 rerun. Tchaikovsky stands at 61.0% with materially lower bassIsRoot than the older 2026-04-09 checkpoints.
Official direct-corpus baseline after the fresh 2026-04-11 rerun. Grieg improves to 60.7% and replaces the earlier 2026-04-09 post-fix checkpoint.

Official direct-corpus baseline after the comparator refresh. The earlier 26.7% Mozart figure was a comparator artifact caused by ignoring relativeroot on applied chords; the refreshed baseline is 61.8% across 54 processed movements.

Official direct-corpus baseline after the DCML comparator refresh now applies relativeroot to applied chords. Chopin rises from the earlier 57.5% checkpoint family to 71.6%. One score is still missing a DCML TSV file.

Official direct-corpus baseline after the comparator refresh and final no-third inversion gating. Corelli reaches 70.3%, bassIsRoot falls to 41.6% of disagreements, and op01n08d is now 11/13 on aligned rows.
Official direct-corpus baseline after the fresh 2026-04-11 rerun. Bach En/Fr Suites settle at 52.4%; many two-voice dance movements remain deferred until mixed-texture orchestration exists.
